LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT LYFT, INC.'S AMENDED MOTION TO FILE AND KEEP CONFIDENTIAL MATERIALS UNDER SEAL. NO APPEARANCES MADE IN THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. RESPONDENT LYFT, INC.'S AMENDED MOTION TO SEAL CERTAIN INFORMATION PROVIDED IN THE PAPERS FILED ON THE PETITIONS TO VACATE AND CONFIRM THE ARBITRATION AWARD IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED AND LYFT HAS SHOWN THAT THE NARROWED SCOPE OF INFORMATION THAT IT SEEKS TO KEEP FROM THE PUBLIC WAS NOT GERMANE TO THE ISSUES ON THE PETITIONS AND THERE ARE OVERRIDING INTERESTS IN THAT INFORMATION WHICH WOULD BE PREJUDICED IF THIS MOTION WERE DENIED. COUNSEL FOR LYFT IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH PRECISELY IDENTIFIES THE INFORMATION THAT IS TO BE SEALED AND COMPLIES WITH CRC 2.550(D) AND (E).JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT LYFT, INC.'S CROSS-PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD IS GRANTED. THE ARBITRATION AWARD ENTITLED "FINAL AWARD" IN JAMS ARBITRATION CASE REFERENCE NO. 1100085960 DATED MAY 3. 2018. BECAUSE THE COURT HAS REJECTED THE PETITION OF PETITIONER TOKIO MARINE SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY TO VACATE THE ARBITRATION AWARD AND THERE ARE NO OTHER GROUNDS THAT HAVE BEEN IDENTIFIED BY TOKIO THAT THE AWARD SHOULD NOT BE CONFIRMED, LYFT IS ENTITLED TO HAVE THE AWARD CONFIRMED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To File And Keep Confidential Materials Under Seal
Off Calendar Aug-28-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RESPONDENT LYFT, INC.'S AMENDED MOTION TO FILE AND KEEP CONFIDENTIAL MATERIALS UNDER SEAL.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Respondent Lyft, Inc.'s amended motion to seal certain information provided in the papers filed on the petitions to vacate and confirm the arbitration award is granted. No opposition filed and Lyft has shown that the narrowed scope of information that it seeks to keep from the public was not germane to the issues on the petitions and there are overriding interests in that information which would be prejudiced if this motion were denied. Counsel for Lyft is required to prepare a proposed order which precisely identifies the information that is to be sealed and complies with CRC 2.550(d) and (e).

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (302/HK)

Lyft, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Second Motion To File And Keep Confidential Materials Under Seal; Memorandum In Support
Off Calendar Aug-08-2018 Off Calendar

Petitioner Tokio Marine Specialty Ins. Co Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities ***Redacted*** (Added For Tentative Ruling Entry Purposes Only)
Off Calendar Aug-08-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Petitioner Tokio Marine Specialty Ins. Co Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award is a duplicate entry.

Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities ***Redacted***
Off Calendar Aug-08-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty.James C. Nielsen of Nielsen, Haley & Abbott LLP, Tel. No. 1-415-248-0162
appearing for the Petitioner;
Atty. C. Brandon Wisoff & Atty. Matthew Cate of Farella Braun & Martel LLP, Tel. No.
1-415-954-4400 appearing for the Respondent Lyft Inc.

RULING - A R G U E D. Petitioner Tokio Marine Specialty Insurance Company's petition to vacate the arbitration award is denied. The critical issue on this petition is not whether the arbitrators (totally) failed to address and resolve Tokio's argument that the auto exclusion applied and thus there was no coverage under the parties' policy for the injuries assertedly suffered by Mr. Kim because Mr. Asresse was a Lyft employee, but why the arbitrators chose not to address and resolve Tokio's argument. (See word minutes for details) Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk

Notice Of Hearing On Cross-Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Aug-08-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. James C. Nielsen of Nielsen, Haley & Abbott LLP, Tel. No. 1-415-248-0162
appearing for the Petitioner;
Atty. C. Brandon Wisoff & Atty. Matthew Cate of Farella Braun & Martel LLP, Tel. No.
1-415-954-4400 appearing for the Respondent Lyft Inc.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Respondent Lyft, Inc.'s cross-petition to confirm arbitration award is granted. The arbitration award entitled "Final Award" in JAMS Arbitration Case Reference No. 1100085960 dated May 3. 2018. Because the court has rejected the petition of petitioner Tokio Marine Specialty Insurance Company to vacate the arbitration award and there are no other grounds that have been identified by Tokio that the award should not be confirmed, Lyft is entitled to have the award confirmed. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion And Motion To File And Keep Confidential Materials Under Seal
Off Calendar Aug-08-2018 Continued To Aug-28-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty.James C. Nielsen of Nielsen, Haley & Abbott LLP, Tel. No. 1-415-248-0162
appearing for the Petitioner;
Atty, C. Brandon Wisoff & Atty. Matthew Cate of farella Braun & Martel LLP, Tel. No.
1-415-954-4400 appearing for the Respondent Lyft Inc.

RULING - The matter was discussed - Respondent Lyft, Inc.'s motion to file and keep confidential materials under seal is continued to August 28, 2018 in order for the moving party to rewrite the motion which was not fully developed in its current motion. Supplemental moving papers to be filed by August 17, 2018. Petitioner may file a Response by August 21, 2018. On August 15, 2018, moving party to e-mail the court identifying the documents that needs to be sealed and the rest of the documents that needs to be filed in the public record.
The August 27 motion to seal hearing is taken off calendar by the moving party. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk
